title: Points to ask for something or get help
description: Points to a toy, food, milk cup, or object to ask for something or get help, such as pointing at an item (ishaara) and looking back at the caregiver to confirm they see it.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: Eli pointed to a toy to show me what he wants and looks back.
He pointed to the cookie on the counter to ask for it.
Kuch lene ke liye ishaara karta hai.

title: Looks at familiar object when named
description: Looks at a familiar object (like a dog, cup, ball, or toy car) when named by a parent or caregiver, such as turning head or looking towards the item when spoken to (bolne par dekhta hai).

sources.title: AAP Clinician Evidence Guide

example_observations: When I said 'where is the ball?', he looked directly at the ball.
He turned his head to look at his bottle when I named it.
Bottle bolne par bottle ki taraf dekha.

title: Follows simple one-step verbal directions
description: Follows simple one-step verbal directions or instructions with or without gestures, such as 'come here' (paas aao), 'give it to me', or handing a toy cup or spoon when requested.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He handed me the toy when I put my hand out and said 'give it to me'.
He came over when I waved my hand and said 'come here'.
Ghar ke andar aao bolne par andar aa gaya.

title: Uses several single words besides mama or dada
description: Uses several single words besides mama or dada, such as saying 'ball', 'cup', 'car', 'didi', or other individual words clearly to communicate.

sources.title: AAP Clinician Evidence Guide

example_observations: He clearly said 'cup' and 'dog' while pointing.
She makes sound attempts at words like 'car' and 'ba' for ball.
Didi bola jab usne behan ko dekha.

title: Plays simple interactive games
description: Plays simple interactive games with caregivers or peers, such as peek-a-boo (hiding eyes and laughing), pat-a-cake, or rolling a rubber ball back and forth.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He laughed and covered his eyes playing peek-a-boo with me.
We rolled the red ball back and forth on the carpet.
Peek-a-boo khelne par hasta hai.

title: Shows affection to familiar people
description: Shows affection to familiar people, such as hugging (gale lagana), kissing, or smiling at parents, siblings, or familiar caregivers when they arrive.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: She gave her father a big hug when he came home.
He smiled warmly and patted my face while being held.
Mummy ko dekhkar smile kiya aur hug kiya.

title: Looks at caregiver for joint attention
description: Looks at caregiver for joint attention, looking at an object (like a bird, plane, or toy) the parent points to, and then looking back at the parent's face (chehra) or eyes to share attention.

sources.title: AAP Clinician Evidence Guide

example_observations: I pointed to a bird outside, and he looked at it, then looked at my face.
He followed my gaze when I looked at the ceiling.
Mera face dekha jab maine chidiya ki taraf dekha.

title: Explores objects by physical manipulation
description: Explores objects by physical manipulation, such as shaking keys, banging toys together, dropping a spoon, or throwing items to see them fall.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He banged the two plastic cups together on the high chair.
She repeatedly dropped her spoon to watch it hit the floor.
Toy ko jor-jor se patak raha tha.

title: Puts small objects into containers
description: Puts small objects into containers, such as placing blocks, keys, or toys inside a cup, box (dabba), or bucket and emptying them out.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He put three small blocks into a plastic cup.
She dropped the keys into the metal bowl and then emptied it.
Dabbe me block daal raha hai.

title: Tries to use items for their intended purpose
description: Tries to use items for their intended purpose, such as holding a toy phone to the ear, holding a toy receiver to the ear, pretending to talk on a toy phone, using a toy telephone appropriately, using a spoon for eating, or using a brush or comb on hair.

sources.title: AAP Clinician Evidence Guide

example_observations: He put the toy phone to his ear and babbled.
She tried to comb her hair with the baby brush.
Phone kaan par lagaya jaise baat kar raha ho.

title: Walks forward independently
description: Walks forward independently without holding onto furniture, walls, or hands, taking steps across the room (bina sahare ke chalna).

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He took six steps across the rug to reach his father.
She is walking around the living room independently now.
Bina sahare ke chalne laga hai.

title: Pulls self up to stand using support
description: Pulls self up to stand using support, pulling up from a sitting or crawling position by holding onto a table, couch, or sofa.

sources.title: AAP Clinician Evidence Guide

example_observations: He pulled himself up using the coffee table to stand.
She grabs the crib rails and stands up on her own.
Sofa pakad kar khada ho jata hai.

title: Climbs onto low furniture or steps
description: Climbs onto low furniture or steps without help, such as climbing up onto a low couch, child chair, or low step cushions.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He crawled and climbed up onto the low couch cushions.
She managed to climb onto the kid-sized armchair.
Sofa par chadne ki koshish ki.

title: Uses neat pincer grasp to pick items
description: Uses neat pincer grasp to pick items, pinching small cereal (cheerios), grains (daane), or small toys using the tips of the thumb (angutha) and index finger (ungli).

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: She picked up a single piece of cheerios cereal using her thumb and pointer finger.
He precisely pinched the tiny wooden peg from the floor.
Anguthe aur ungli se dana uthaya.

title: Scribbles spontaneously on surfaces
description: Scribbles spontaneously on surfaces, using a crayon, pencil, or marker to make random marks or scribbled lines on paper.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He made thick red scribbles on the sheet of paper.
She grabbed the marker and drew lines on the cardboard.
Crayon pakad kar paper par line banai.

title: Turns pages of a book manually
description: Turns pages of a book manually, turning cardboard or paper pages of a book (kitab) one by one or several at a time when looking at pictures.

sources.title: AAP Clinician Evidence Guide

example_observations: He flipped through the board book, turning two pages at once.
She loves turning the pages when we read together.
Kitab ke panne palat raha tha.

title: Says two-word descriptive phrases
description: Says two-word descriptive phrases (excluding repetitions), combining two words to communicate, like 'more milk', 'go outside', 'daddy car', or 'papa aao'.

sources.title: AAP Clinician Evidence Guide

example_observations: She said 'more milk' while pointing to her glass.
He combined two words saying 'daddy car' when his dad arrived.
Papa aao bola jab papa gate par the.

title: Points to picture in book when asked
description: Points to picture in book when asked (like 'where is the dog?' or 'where is the ball?'), pointing to drawings of animals, cars, or food in pages.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He pointed to the cow in the picture book when I asked.
She pointed to the puppy picture while we were reading.
Kitab me haathi par ungli rakh kar dikhaya.

title: Points to at least two body parts
description: Points to at least two body parts (like nose, eyes, hair, aankh, kaan) when asked, touching them during a body parts naming game.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: She pointed to her nose when I said 'where is your nose?'.
He touched his eyes and ears when we sang the song.
Aankh kahan hai bolne par aankh dikhai.

title: Uses physical gestures along with words
description: Uses physical gestures along with words, combining actions like waving goodbye (haath hilana) and saying 'bye', or nodding head and saying 'yes'.

sources.title: AAP Clinician Evidence Guide

example_observations: He waved goodbye and said 'bye' to his grandmother.
She nodded vigorously and said 'yes' when offered fruit.
Haath hila kar bye bola.

title: Imitates other children during play
description: Imitates other children during play, copying actions of peers, such as running after them in the park, copying block stacking, or imitating play.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He started running around the playground copying the older boys.
She stacked blocks because she saw another child doing it.
Dusre bacche ko dekh kar waisa hi karne laga.

title: Shows concern for a crying child
description: Shows concern for a crying child, noticing when a friend (dost) or sibling is hurt or sad, looking at them, or offering a toy (khilona) to comfort them.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He walked over and offered his teddy bear to his crying sister.
She looked sad and patted her friend who was crying.
Rote hue bacche ko dekh kar udas ho gaya.

title: Looks at parent face for reaction
description: Looks at parent face for reaction (social referencing) in new or unfamiliar situations to check if it is safe to touch a toy, meet a stranger, or climb.

sources.title: AAP Clinician Evidence Guide

example_observations: He looked at my face when a loud toy started playing before touching it.
She looked at me to check if it was safe to climb the slide.
Ajnabee ko dekh kar mummy ke chehre ki taraf dekha.

title: Sorts objects by shapes or colors
description: Sorts objects by shapes or colors, grouping blocks, rings, or toys into separate piles by color (like red, blue, green) or shapes.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He sorted the plastic rings into piles of red and blue.
She put the square blocks into the square slot of the shape sorter.
Lal rang ke blocks ko alag kiya.

title: Plays with simple mechanical toys
description: Plays with simple mechanical toys that have buttons, levers, or moving parts, such as pushing piano keys to hear sounds or sliding buttons on a pop-up toy.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He pressed the piano keys repeatedly to hear the musical notes.
She figured out how to slide the button to make the toy animal pop up.
Piano ke button daba kar gana sun raha tha.

title: Finds hidden objects under covers
description: Finds hidden objects under covers, searching for and finding a toy or block hidden under a blanket, pillow, or cup.

sources.title: AAP Clinician Evidence Guide

example_observations: He lifted the blanket and the cushion to find his hidden toy car.
She looked under the cup where I had hidden the block.
Chupaye hue khilone ko dhundh liya.

title: Kicks a ball forward easily
description: Kicks a ball forward easily using one foot without losing balance or falling over, propelling a ball in the yard.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He kicked the soccer ball across the yard successfully.
She ran up and kicked the soft ball forward.
Ball ko laat mari bina gire.

title: Runs easily without falling down
description: Runs easily without falling down, running fast in the park or playground, changing directions or speed safely without tripping (bina thokar khaye).

sources.title: AAP Clinician Evidence Guide

example_observations: He runs easily around the kitchen island without tripping.
She ran to catch up with the dog in the yard.
Park me tezi se bhaag raha tha.

title: Walks up steps with assistance
description: Walks up steps with assistance, climbing stairs by holding onto a handrail or caregiver's hand for balance.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He walked up three steps while holding onto my hand.
She used the handrail to climb up the porch steps.
Haath pakad kar seedhi chadhne laga.

title: Builds tower of at least 4 blocks
description: Builds tower of at least 4 blocks, stacking four or more wooden blocks or Legos on top of each other without the tower falling.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He stacked four wooden blocks on top of each other before it fell.
She built a high tower using six building blocks.
Char blocks ek ke upar ek rakh diye.

title: Uses spoon to feed self partially
description: Uses spoon to feed self partially, scooping food (like yogurt or dahi) and eating with a spoon, even if spilling some.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He scooped up yogurt and successfully fed himself using the spoon.
She eats her oatmeal using a plastic spoon independently.
Chammach se khana khane ki koshish ki.

title: Unwraps packages or opens lids
description: Unwraps packages or opens lids, such as opening a box lid to find a toy, tearing wrapping paper off a gift, or removing container caps.

sources.title: AAP Clinician Evidence Guide

example_observations: He peeled off the wrapper from his cheese stick.
She opened the lid of the small plastic box to find the toy.
Gift ka wrapper nikal diya.

title: Says sentences of 3 or 4 words
description: Says sentences of 3 or 4 words, combining words to form simple sentences like 'me want cookie', 'daddy go work', or speaking in short phrases.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He said 'me go outside now' when looking at the door.
She said 'where is my teddy?' when she couldn't find it.
Papa office gaye bola.

title: Asks basic who, what, where questions
description: Asks basic who, what, where questions to locate items or people, such as asking 'where doggy go?', 'who is that?', or 'what is that?'.

sources.title: AAP Clinician Evidence Guide

example_observations: She pointed and asked 'what is that?' when seeing a vacuum.
He asked 'where is mummy?' when she left the room.
Mummy kahan hai poochha.

title: Follows instructions with two related steps
description: Follows instructions with two related steps, carrying out two connected actions, like picking up a spoon (chammach) and putting it in a plate, fetching a book and placing it in a box (dabba), putting socks in a laundry basket, picking up socks and placing them in a bin, or placing laundry items into a basket when asked.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He picked up the cup and placed it on the table when asked.
She took off her shoes and put them by the door.
Khilona uthao aur dabbe me rakho bola to kar diya.

title: Identifies common objects in books
description: Identifies common objects in books or pictures by pointing or naming them, like identifying a cat, apple, cup, key, or car when looking at pages.

sources.title: AAP Clinician Evidence Guide

example_observations: She named the cat, tree, and car in the picture book.
He pointed to the car when we looked out the window.
Billi kahan hai bolne par billi dikhai.

title: Plays near other children (parallel play)
description: Plays near other children (parallel play), playing with toys next to another child in a sandbox or playroom without direct interaction or conversation.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He played with cars right next to another boy playing with blocks.
She sat near a friend in the sandbox and occasionally shared a shovel.
Dusre bacche ke paas baith kar apna khel khel raha tha.

title: Displays wide range of distinct emotions
description: Displays wide range of distinct emotions, showing anger (gussa), frustration, happiness, or sadness, such as crying or screaming (shor machana) when a toy breaks.

sources.title: AAP Clinician Evidence Guide

example_observations: She got very frustrated and threw a block when her tower fell.
He laughed with joy when playing tag.
Gusse me khilona fek diya.

title: Greets familiar people spontaneously
description: Greets familiar people spontaneously, waving or saying 'hi' or 'hello' to family members (dada, dadi, papa) or neighbors without prompting.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He waved and said 'hello' to the neighbor when we went outside.
She ran to greet her grandmother at the door with a loud 'hi!'.
Grandpa ko dekhkar 'dadu' bolkar bhaaga.

title: Engages in simple pretend play scenarios
description: Engages in simple pretend play scenarios, using objects symbolically, like pretending a box (dabba) is a car (gadi) or boat, or pretending to feed a doll.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: She put a spoon to her doll's mouth and said 'eat eat'.
He pretended the shoe box was a racing car and made engine sounds.
Gudiya ko khana khila raha tha.

title: Matches identical items or colors
description: Matches identical items or colors, pairing matching items, like matching socks from a laundry basket or pairing identical animal cards.

sources.title: AAP Clinician Evidence Guide

example_observations: He matched the red plastic bear to the red bowl card.
She paired the matching socks from the laundry basket.
Ek jaisi do photos ko ek sath rakha.

title: Draws simple circle closed loop
description: Draws simple circle closed loop, drawing or copying a circular shape or closed loop on paper with a pen, pencil, or crayon (gol aakar banana).

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He drew a rough circular loop on the whiteboard after I demonstrated.
She copied the circle I drew on the paper.
Paper par gol aakar banaya.

title: Jumps off step with both feet
description: Jumps off step with both feet, jumping down from a low step or bench and landing on both feet on the grass or floor.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He stood on the lowest step and jumped off, landing on both feet.
She jumped off the wooden bench onto the grass.
Seedhi se neeche kood gaya.

title: Balances on one foot briefly for 2s
description: Balances on one foot briefly for 2s, standing on one leg for a second or two without support or falling.

sources.title: AAP Clinician Evidence Guide

example_observations: He lifted his left foot and stood on one leg for two seconds.
She briefly balanced on one foot while playing hopscotch.
Ek pair par thodi der khada raha.

title: Pedals a tricycle independently
description: Pedals a tricycle independently, pushing the pedals to ride a small tricycle or three-wheeler cycle.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He pushed the pedals to ride his tricycle down the driveway.
She is learning to pedal her new trike around the patio.
Tricycle chalane laga hai.

title: Builds tower of 6 to 8 blocks
description: Builds tower of 6 to 8 blocks, balancing and stacking six to eight building blocks to make a high tower without it falling.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He carefully stacked 8 colorful blocks to build a tower.
She successfully placed the 7th block on top of the stack.
7 blocks ki tower banai bina giraye.

title: Uses child-safe scissors for paper
description: Uses child-safe scissors for paper, snipping or cutting paper edges with safety scissors, opening and closing them with finger control.

sources.title: AAP Clinician Evidence Guide

example_observations: She snipped the edges of the construction paper with toy scissors.
He tried cutting the paper strip in half.
Safety scissors se paper katne laga.

title: Screws and unscrews jar lids
description: Screws and unscrews jar lids, twisting caps off water bottles, opening jar lids, or turning toy nuts and bolts.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He twisted the lid off the peanut butter jar.
She screwed the cap back onto her water bottle.
Dabbe ka dhakkan khol diya.

title: Asks why, how, and when questions
description: Asks why, how, and when questions to understand things, like asking how a car works, why someone is sad, or when they are going.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He asked 'why is the grass green?' while walking.
She asked 'how does the car work?' when we started driving.
Aisa kyun hota hai poocha.

title: Uses sentences of 4 or 5 words
description: Uses sentences of 4 or 5 words, speaking in longer sentences (like 'I want to play block game' or 'my dog is big') understandable to visitors or strangers.

sources.title: AAP Clinician Evidence Guide

example_observations: She said 'I want to go play outside' to the visitor.
He said 'look at the big airplane' pointing upward.
Mujhe khilona chahiye bola.

title: Recounts a simple story or event
description: Recounts a simple story or event from the day, describing activities, like 'went to park, played slide' or telling a simple narrative.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: She told me 'we go store, buy apple' when she came home.
He narrated a story about a bear eating honey.
Park me kya kiya wo bataya.

title: Identifies colors and basic shapes by name
description: Identifies colors and basic shapes by name, pointing to and naming red, blue, green, circle, or square blocks.

sources.title: AAP Clinician Evidence Guide

example_observations: He correctly called the blocks 'red', 'yellow', and 'green'.
She pointed to a square and named it correctly.
Lal aur peela rang pehchan kar bataya.

title: Plays cooperatively taking turns
description: Plays cooperatively taking turns, sharing toys (like cars) and taking turns on slides or in group games with other children (bari-bari khelna).

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: They took turns sliding down the slide at the playground.
She shared her markers and worked on a joint drawing with her friend.
Bacho ke sath bari-bari khelta hai.

title: Acts out complex pretend scenarios
description: Acts out complex pretend scenarios, creating imaginative roleplay with roles (doctor checking doll's heartbeat, teaching stuffed toys, or playing house).

sources.title: AAP Clinician Evidence Guide

example_observations: He pretended to be a doctor, checking his teddy bear's heartbeat with a toy stethoscope.
She played house and acted as the mother cooking food.
Doctor-doctor khel raha tha.

title: Tries to comfort distressed family or friends
description: Tries to comfort distressed family or friends, hugging or consoling someone who is sad, hurt, or crying (rote dekh kar hug karna).

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He patted his friend's shoulder when the friend fell on the playground.
She hugged her mother and said 'don't cry' when she saw her sad.
Dost ko chot lagne par chup karaya.

title: Draws simple person with 2 to 4 parts
description: Draws simple person with 2 to 4 parts, sketching a stick figure with a head (circle) and legs or arms.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He drew a circle for a head and two lines for legs, calling it 'daddy'.
She drew a stick figure with a head, body, and arms.
Chehra aur haath-pair ki drawing banai.

title: Correctly matches same and different items
description: Correctly matches same and different items, comparing objects to point out which ones are identical and which ones are different.

sources.title: AAP Clinician Evidence Guide

example_observations: She pointed to two identical cards and said 'these are the same'.
He pointed to a blue ball among red ones and said 'this is different'.
Dono alag-alag hain bataya.

title: Demonstrates basic time concepts understanding
description: Demonstrates basic time concepts understanding, knowing words like morning, night, today, tomorrow (kal), or yesterday.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He said 'we go to park tomorrow' when talking about our plans.
She said 'it is night, time for sleep' when the sun set.
Raat ho gayi so jao bola.

title: Hops on one foot a few times
description: Hops on one foot a few times, hopping forward on one leg three or more times without losing balance.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He hopped on his right foot three times across the room.
She is practicing hopping on one leg on the grass.
Ek pair par kood-kood kar chalne laga.

title: Catches a bounced ball most of the time
description: Catches a bounced ball most of the time, catching a large ball with both hands and arms after it bounces off the floor.

sources.title: AAP Clinician Evidence Guide

example_observations: He caught the playground ball after it bounced once.
She successfully caught the large blue ball bounced by her brother.
Didi ki feki hui ball lapak li.

title: Climbs stairs alternating feet without support
description: Climbs stairs alternating feet without support, walking up stairs placing one foot on each step normally without holding a rail.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He climbed the stairs alternating his feet, left then right, on each step.
She went up the porch stairs walking normally like an adult.
Seedhi par bari-bari se pair rakh kar chadha.

title: Cuts paper along straight line with scissors
description: Cuts paper along straight line with scissors, cutting a sheet of paper in half along a straight line with safety scissors.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: She cut along the black line on the sheet of paper with safety scissors.
He successfully cut the paper strip in half along the crease.
Kagaz ko seedhi line me kaat diya.

title: Copies simple capital letters
description: Copies simple capital letters, drawing or copying alphabet letters like T, H, or V on paper with a crayon or pencil.

sources.title: AAP Clinician Evidence Guide

example_observations: He wrote the letter 'T' on his drawing sheet.
She traced the letter 'H' after I wrote it for her.
Alphabet T likhne ki koshish ki.

title: Buttons large buttons independently
description: Buttons large buttons independently, buttoning one or two large buttons on a sweater, shirt, or jacket.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He managed to slide the large button through the slot on his sweater.
She buttoned the coat buttons independently.
Shirt ka bada button laga liya.

title: Speaks clearly in full sentences
description: Speaks clearly in full sentences of 5 or more words (understandable to anyone), communicating thoughts without speech hesitation or stuttering (bina atkan).

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He said 'I want to go play with my friend at the park today'.
She spoke clearly telling the doctor what her toy was doing.
Mujhe dosto ke sath khelna hai bolkar bahar gaya.

title: Tells longer stories with detail
description: Tells longer stories with detail, describing a complex event or school day activity (like explaining what they did in drawing class or art class).

sources.title: AAP Clinician Evidence Guide

example_observations: She told a detailed story about how the cat chased a squirrel up a tree.
He recounted his school day, explaining what they did in art class.
School me kya hua pure detail me bataya.

title: Uses future tense correctly
description: Uses future tense correctly, saying words like 'will go', 'going to play', or 'tomorrow' (kal) when talking about future plans.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: She said 'tomorrow we will go swim in the pool'.
He said 'I am going to build a spaceship later'.
Kal hum nani ke ghar jayenge bola.

title: Follows complex multi-step directions
description: Follows complex multi-step directions, carrying out a sequence of instructions (like going to the room, getting a bag, and waiting at the gate).

sources.title: AAP Clinician Evidence Guide

example_observations: He went to his room, retrieved his coat, put it on, and waited by the door.
She put away her drawing, washed her hands, and sat down at the table.
Khilona rakho, haath dhoo kar khana khao bola to sab kiya.

title: Follows rules in group games
description: Follows rules in group games, cooperating and following rules in games like hide-and-seek, tag, or simple board games.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He waited his turn while playing a board game with his cousins.
She played hide-and-seek, following the counting and hiding rules.
Chhupan-chhai khelne me bari ka wait kiya.

title: Shows independence from parents confidently
description: Shows independence from parents confidently, waving goodbye and joining preschool or staying at a party without crying or asking for parents.

sources.title: AAP Clinician Evidence Guide

example_observations: She waved goodbye and ran to join her class at preschool.
He stayed at his friend's birthday party without asking for his mother.
School chhodne par bina roye andar chala gaya.

title: Distinguishes real vs make-believe accurately
description: Distinguishes real vs make-believe accurately, knowing that dragons, fairies, or superheroes are pretend and not real.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He said 'superheroes are pretend, they cannot really fly'.
She explained that the talking dragon in the book wasn't real.
Bhoot sach me nahi hote bola.

title: Draws person with at least 6 parts
description: Draws person with at least 6 parts, sketching a figure that includes a head, body (torso), arms, legs, eyes, and fingers.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He drew a picture of himself with head, body, arms, legs, ears, and hands.
She drew her teacher including hair, eyes, nose, mouth, body, and feet.
Mummy ki drawing banai jisme haath, pair, chehra, aankh sab the.

title: Writes simple numbers and letters
description: Writes simple numbers and letters, copying digits 1 to 5 or letters of their name on paper.

sources.title: AAP Clinician Evidence Guide

example_observations: He wrote the numbers 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 on the blackboard.
She printed her own name on her drawing sheet.
Apna naam aur 1 se 5 tak ginti likhi.

title: Counts ten or more objects correctly
description: Counts ten or more objects correctly, pointing to blocks, toys, or raisins (kishmish) one by one as they count up to ten or twelve.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He counted ten toy cars lined up on the rug, pointing to each one.
She counted twelve apple slices on her plate.
Plate me rakhi barah kishmish gini.

title: Skips and hops alternating feet
description: Skips and hops alternating feet, hopping forward on alternating legs with coordination while moving.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He skipped down the sidewalk alternating his feet with ease.
She hopped dynamically on alternate legs during physical education.
Sidewalk par koodte-bhaagte hue gaya.

title: Swings and climbs on play structures
description: Swings and climbs on play structures, swinging on a swing independently (pumping legs) or climbing monkey bars or jungle gyms.

sources.title: AAP Clinician Evidence Guide

example_observations: He pumped his legs to swing high on the swing set independently.
She climbed across the monkey bars without help.
Monkey bars par bina madad ke chadh gaya.

title: Stands on one foot for 10s or more
description: Stands on one foot for 10s or more, balancing on one leg for ten seconds or longer without touching down.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He stood on his left leg and counted to ten without losing balance.
She balanced on one foot for twelve seconds during the game.
Ek pair par das second tak khada raha.

title: Copies a triangle shape correctly
description: Copies a triangle shape correctly, drawing or copying a shape with three distinct sides and corners.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He drew a triangle shape on paper after looking at a drawing.
She traced and drew three triangles in a row.
Paper par tikona/triangle banaya.

title: Uses fork and table knife to eat
description: Uses fork and table knife to eat, using a fork to scoop food and trying to cut soft fruit with a table knife.

sources.title: AAP Clinician Evidence Guide

example_observations: He used a fork to pick up his pasta noodles.
She held a table knife to try and cut her soft banana slices.
Chammach aur kante se pasta khaya.

title: Tries to tie knots or thread laces
description: Tries to tie knots or thread laces, practicing threading laces through shoe holes or attempting to tie a single knot.

sources.title: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early.

example_observations: He successfully tied a simple single knot in a piece of yarn.
She is practicing threading her shoe laces through the holes.
Khilono ki dori me gaanth lagai.
